---
title: "Bistability and affect shift dynamics in the prediction of psychological well-being"
author:
  - name:
      given: C.
      family: Goicoechea
  - name:
      given: V.
      family: Dakos
  - name:
      given: D.
      family: Sanabria
  - name:
      given: S.
      family: Heshmati
  - name:
      given: M.
      family: Westhoff
  - name:
      given: O.
      family: Banos
  - name:
      given: H.
      family: Pomares
  - name:
      given: S.G.
      family: Hofmann
  - name:
      given: P.
      family: Perakakis
date: "2025"
citation:
  type: article-journal
  container-title: "Emotion"
  doi: 10.1037/emo0001454
  url: https://pandelisperakakis.info/research/affect-dynamics/papers/bistability-affect-shift-dynamics.html
  pdf-url: https://pandelisperakakis.info/research/affect-dynamics/assets/Goicoechea_2024_Bistability_and_affect_shift_dynamics.pdf
  issued: 2025
  volume: 25
  issue: 4
  page: 982-996
google-scholar: true
toc: false
---

Author-accepted manuscript, self-archived on this server. The version of record is published in *Emotion*.

We show that bistability is a prevalent phenomenon in human affect dynamics and develop 10 novel metrics to characterise affect shift dynamics. The Positive-to-Negative Affect Shift Ratio (P2N-ASR) is a better predictor of psychological well-being compared to traditional intensity metrics.

[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Download PDF](../assets/Goicoechea_2024_Bistability_and_affect_shift_dynamics.pdf){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm target="_blank"}

```{=html}
<object data="../assets/Goicoechea_2024_Bistability_and_affect_shift_dynamics.pdf" type="application/pdf" width="100%" height="800" style="margin-top:1.5rem;border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color);">
  <p>Your browser cannot display the embedded PDF. <a href="../assets/Goicoechea_2024_Bistability_and_affect_shift_dynamics.pdf">Download it here</a>.</p>
</object>
```
